entry.title: JoongAng Daily: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • GS Entec acquiring monopile technology from Sif Netherlands
  • Gasoline prices fall for a fourth straight week in Korea
  • Mayor Oh visits, praises Singapore's public housing project
  • Tennis wunderkind Cho Se-hyuk does it again — this time in Germany
  • Woo Sang-hyeok takes No. 1 spot on high jump world ranking
  • Lollapalooza 'Magic'
  • Kim Min-jae says he's just a young defender looking to give it his all
  • Yoon's approval rating falls to a record-low 28 percent
  • Hwang Ui-jo appears in Bordeaux's first Ligue 2 game
  • [CELEB] Lim Yoon-a sings, dances, acts and juggles two careers at once
  • [YOUR PLAYLIST] Two girl groups later, Hezz is rebranding her solo career
The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: Guardian: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live: drone hits Russia’s Black Sea fleet HQ; Zelenskiy urges civilians to leave Donetsk
  • Kansas referendum will test change in abortion landscape since Roe fell
  • Don’t buy the Stonewall line on gender identity? Fine. You can’t be sacked for that now | Sonia Sodha
  • Ukrainian offensive forces Russia to bolster troops in occupied south
  • Booming US cannabis industry seen as fertile ground for union expansion
  • ‘People are worried it will happen again’: the English village whose water ran out
  • If education is all about getting a job, the humanities are left just to the rich | Kenan Malik
  • The Big Lie review: Jonathan Lemire laments what Trump hath wrought
  • Teller of the Unexpected by Matthew Dennison review – the tall tales of a big kid
  • ‘A shark bit my arm and leg off. Now I want to save the species’
  • Tory MP urged to quit job as adviser to ‘climate denier’ US fossil fuel firm

  • Spain ‘beach bodies’ ad edited out my prosthetic leg, says British model

  • Henrik Stenson leads LIV event but Donald Trump circus steals limelight

  • Scott powers home to beat Dean to 200m freestyle gold for Scotland
  • Prince Charles accepted £1m from family of Osama bin Laden, report claims
  • Evacuations ordered in northern California after new wildfire breaks out

  • Fearless England hold final piece of puzzle to complete long road to glory | Suzanne Wrack
  • Relentless Popp is a constant danger – but Lionesses can cause problems of their own | Anita Asante

  • ‘Women’s football and society has changed’: Williamson hails Euro impact

  • Salah and Núñez seal Community Shield glory as Liverpool sink Manchester City
  • Alarm grows as Orban prepares to take ‘pure Nazi’ rhetoric to US
  • Russia’s Gazprom halts gas supplies to Latvia
  • China conducts military exercises off Taiwan after warning Pelosi to scrap visit
  • Van Vleuten roars into lead at Tour de France Femmes with stage seven win
  • Jared Kushner: I stopped Trump attacking Murdoch in 2015
  • George Russell claims first pole of his career at Hungarian Grand Prix
  • ‘Soon it will be unrecognisable’: total climate meltdown cannot be stopped, says expert
  • Mark Diacono’s recipes for alcohol-free summer drinks
  • Like Bill Gates before him, Mark Zuckerberg is having a ‘Pearl Harbour’ moment | John Naughton
  • Catastrophic flash flooding kills 25 in Kentucky and at least a dozen missing
  • House-passed assault weapons ban appears to be doomed in the Senate
  • Fire of Love review – a true tale of volcanic passions
  • Chris Rock jokes about slap after Will Smith apology video

  • Phoebe Bridgers review – a truth-telling phenomenon

  • Kim Wilde looks back: ‘I was inspired by photos of Lauren Bacall’
  • Protesters storm Iraq parliament again amid unrest over Iran-backed groups

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 31 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 31 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-31 07:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Parents of critically injured Mirror dancer arrive in Hong Kong, expected to visit son in hospital on Sunday afternoon
  • Will sharp rises in interest rates diminish Hong Kong’s love affair with property?
  • Hong Kong protests: Oscar-winning director Malcolm Clarke releases documentary series offering different take on 2019 unrest, says he is not ‘working for the Chinese government’
  • Hong Kong’s Star Ferry on the brink of closure? No way, says company
  • Tiger Global-backed Hong Kong start-up SleekFlow targets US$300 million valuation as social e-commerce grows
  • Hong Kong must brace for geopolitical ‘worst-case scenario’, finance chief warns, with over 150 Chinese firms at risk of US delisting
  • 100 tonnes of frozen meat smuggled into mainland China each night: Hong Kong steps up action against cross-border crime comeback
  • Hong Kong to explore ‘all options’ on land supply, including use of green belts, John Lee says as environment chief points to development balance
  • Hong Kong buyers snap up all Novo Land flats on offer in pre-emptive move ahead of city’s first prime rate increase in four years
  • Hong Kong’s John Lee conducts first community walkabout in Sham Shui Po. What did he discuss with residents and shop operators?
  • New York-based Penetron develops concrete solutions to reduce carbon footprint of buildings and infrastructure in Hong Kong and China
  • China posts shock contraction in factory activity for July despite economic rebound
  • China using its new Global Security Initiative to build military standing in Africa
  • China, Japan to hold in-person foreign ministerial talks on sidelines of Asean meeting in Cambodia next week: sources
  • US Navy using ‘China threat theory’ to fund expansion plans, Chinese analysts say
  • Debris from China’s Long March 5B rocket crashes back to Earth over Indian Ocean
  • China puts on show of naval force ahead of Nancy Pelosi’s possible Taiwan visit
  • China’s official media sends indirect warnings to Nancy Pelosi over Taiwan visit
  • Youth in India have found a new way to get high; flavoured condoms
  • Indian travellers hoping to escape summer heat face passport, visa woes
  • Why Asia-Pacific governments should let independent news thrive
  • In Thailand, having a regular job could still leave you US$218,000 in debt
  • US-China relations: Asean needs to pay attention to great power rivalry in the Pacific
  • Malaysia’s Tasik Chini risks being stripped of its Unesco status unless decades of damage to ‘dragon god lake’ is undone
  • Member of marauding monkey gang put down by Japanese authorities, reports say
  • Coronavirus: New Zealand border reopens to the world after closing to almost all travellers in 2020
  • India’s Mukesh Ambani streams into race for online viewers as Netflix, Disney+ eye growth market
  • Ukraine war: Russia says it has invited UN, Red Cross experts to probe jail deaths
  • Ukraine war: Lebanon seizes ship carrying ‘stolen’ Ukrainian grain
  • Italy shocked by video of fatal attack on African immigrant
  • Unvaccinated Novak Djokovic still hopes to play at US Open
  • Zelensky orders mandatory evacuations in Ukraine’s Donetsk region as fighting intensifies
  • Coronavirus: Joe Biden again tests positive for Covid-19, returns to isolation
  • In race for monkeypox vaccines, experts see repeat of Covid-19 where wealthy nations have advantage
  • Thousands of Iraqi protesters storm parliament in Baghdad, set up sit-in
  • Elon Musk’s ‘gross’ antics turn owners and would-be buyers against Tesla
  • Catholic prison chaplain caught with drugs hidden in fast food intended for inmates – German report says
  • UK leadership race: Liz Truss surges ahead, wins endorsement from former rival
  • New York declares state emergency as monkeypox infections spread, WHO warns of more deaths to come in Europe
  • Pope calls Canada indigenous abuse ‘genocide’, talks of stepping aside now he’s 85
  • Another global crash on the horizon as the world’s economic engines sputter
  • Dad ‘doused me in kerosene’: Christina Chung on her abusive father, rebellious daughters and her return to modelling at 50
  • ‘It’s not a portrait of me’: Pipilotti Rist, Swiss video artist, on Behind Your Eyelid, her Hong Kong show
  • From the Aztecs to avo, the scoop on ‘avocado’ and ‘guacamole’ – its tropical American origins and how alligator pear entered the mix
  • In war on cancer, new drugs and therapies suggest we are on our way to finding cures
  • Why food portions are getting smaller: ‘shrinkflation’ at restaurants means less on your plate for the same price
  • A change from Jackie Chan and Jet Li – Beast Cops’ Gordon Chan on how directing the Hong Kong gangs vs police film was not like his previous work
  • Cannibalism with casual couplings, human body parts turned into furniture – madness is the new normal in Sayaka Murata’s short stories
  • 7 new K-dramas to watch in August 2022: Netflix’s A Model Family, If You Wish Upon Me, The Law Cafe, and more
  • Why Stranger Things’ Sadie Sink kills it on the red carpet – she embraces Chanel, Gucci, Prada and looks stunning in a blazer
  • Museum of Art and Photography in Bangalore, India, opening soon, will tell the story of Indian art in an entertaining way, founder says
  • Inside luxury fashion’s sustainability evolution: Gucci, Louis Vuitton and Dior are offering eco-friendly designs, with celebrities from Billie Eilish to Emma Stone voicing their support
  • Donald Trump’s 4 biggest tantrums: Piers Morgan said he behaved like The Godfather, Sacha Baron Cohen was cut off mid-interview, and a former White House aide even said he smashed plates
  • 10 celebrities estranged from their kids: Elon Musk’s daughter changed her name, Caitlyn Jenner doesn’t keep up with the Kardashians, Suri doesn’t see Tom Cruise and Maddox doesn’t speak to Brad Pitt
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
